title: RC tag CI stabilization: deterministic holds-race negative control + honest Windows-portable subset
action: (1) scripts/test_worktree_holds_race.mjs scenario (c): the negative control expects the OLD check-then-act flow to double-grant the same path to 2+ of 8 racers, but on slow 2-core CI runners the racers serialize naturally and only 1 wins (DETECTOR DID NOT BITE on Linux CI Node 18/20/22). Make it deterministic with the same technique 1710-1 used for test_claim_race: a barrier so ALL racers complete the findForeignHolds conflict check (seeing an empty ledger) BEFORE ANY racer performs its reserve+mirrorHold writes — then the double-grant is structural, not timing-dependent. Verify 10/10 consecutive local runs. (2) .github/workflows/windows.yml: the windows-portable subset currently includes 4 suites that fail with genuine pre-existing Windows portability bugs newly exposed by actually running there (test_bee_cli — worktree reverse gitdir pointer C:\ path mismatch; test_bee_write_guard_hook; test_cells; test_misc). Exclude exactly these 4 from the subset with a LOUD comment block naming each excluded suite and the reason + pointing at the P1 backlog row (the orchestrator files it). Do not exclude anything else; the remaining suites must still run and the zero-match filter guard stays.

truths: windows.yml exclusion is explicit and annotated, nothing else excluded
prohibitions: Do not weaken scenario (d) (the fixed-flow one-winner assertion)
Do not silently drop suites — every exclusion is named with a reason

trace.outcome: Scenario (c) in test_worktree_holds_race.mjs made deterministic via an fs-based ready-file barrier (same technique test_claim_race.mjs's g-unsafe control uses): every racer signals its findForeignHolds check is done before any racer proceeds to reserve+mirrorHold, so the double-grant is structural instead of timing-dependent. Reproduced the CI RED locally under 'taskset -c 0,1' (1 FAIL in 5 pre-fix runs), then proved 10/10 green post-fix both unconstrained and under the same 2-core constraint. Separately, windows.yml now excludes exactly test_bee_cli.mjs/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs/test_cells.mjs/test_misc.mjs from the windows-portable subset via a new BEE_VERIFY_EXCLUDE env var in run_verify.mjs (auto-add deviation, since no exclusion mechanism existed that wouldn't require a hand-maintained second suite list), with a loud annotated comment block naming each suite, the reason, and the P1 backlog row (2026-07-21, 'ts':'2026-07-21T03:54:02.676Z). Full chain green: node scripts/test_worktree_holds_race.mjs && node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> 54 suites PASS.

deviations.reason: Cell listed only scripts/test_worktree_holds_race.mjs and .github/workflows/windows.yml, but honestly excluding exactly 4 named suites from the windows-portable subset had no existing mechanism to hook into: run_verify.mjs only supported a root-prefix filter (BEE_VERIFY_ROOT_FILTER) and a compile-time, all-callers EXCLUDE set. Reimplementing suite discovery by hand inside windows.yml would recreate the exact hand-maintained-list problem contention-split's auto-discovery (cs-4) just removed. Added a new BEE_VERIFY_EXCLUDE env var (comma-separated exact repo-relative paths, applied after the root filter, own zero-match guard mirroring the existing one) so windows.yml can drop the 4 suites for its own job only, with zero suite-list duplication and the discovered SUITES export (which test_verify_manifest.mjs's mandatory/floor checks read) left completely untouched.

trace.verify_output:
$ node scripts/test_worktree_holds_race.mjs && node scripts/run_verify.mjs
PASS test_worktree_holds_race: (a) 8 real mirrorHold() racers on 8 distinct paths/holders -> all 8 entries survived (no lost update); (b) deliberate-red unguarded proxy lost at least one racer's entry (detector bites, lock removed); (c) OLD check-then-act flow double-granted the SAME path (detector bites, hazard proven real); (d) NEW withHoldsLock-atomic flow on the SAME path yielded exactly 1 winner among 8 racers (no double grant); (e) renewHolds pushed mirrored_at forward, keeping a live session's hold active past its original TTL window, without touching an unrelated session's holds.
...
PASS run_verify: 54 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=57546ms

Additional evidence (worker-run, not part of the cell's literal verify command but proving the fix's determinism claim):
- test_worktree_holds_race.mjs run 10/10 consecutively green, unconstrained (16-core box).
- test_worktree_holds_race.mjs run 10/10 consecutively green under `taskset -c 0,1` (2-core constraint) — the exact condition that reproduced RED before the fix (4/5 runs under taskset -c 0,1 pre-fix: 1 FAIL with "(c) DETECTOR DID NOT BITE ... got 1").
- Windows-portable subset simulated locally: BEE_VERIFY_ROOT_FILTER=skills/bee-hive/templates/tests BEE_VERIFY_EXCLUDE=<4 named suites> node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 11 suite(s) (exactly root-set-of-15 minus the 4 named exclusions), all green.
- Full unfiltered node scripts/run_verify.mjs (54 suites, no env overrides) -> PASS, includes all 4 "excluded-on-Windows-only" suites passing normally on Linux.
- BEE_VERIFY_EXCLUDE zero-match guard verified: excluding every discovered suite under the root filter correctly refuses with exit 1 ("excluded every remaining suite — refusing a silent trivial-green run").
